task_completion.description: OpenBCI GUI application is open and visible
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screenshot shows the OpenBCI GUI application window.

task_completion.description: Ganglion board is selected as the data source
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the word 'Ganglion' in the top-left status area of the OpenBCI GUI or within the System Control Panel settings.
Look for the word 'Synthetic' in the top-left status area of the OpenBCI GUI or within the System Control Panel settings.

task_completion.description: The data session has been started
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Stop Data Stream' button at the top of the GUI, or visible waveforms actively plotting in the widgets, indicating the session is active.

task_completion.description: Time Series widget displays exactly 4 channels
task_completion.visual_evidence: Examine the 'Time Series' widget. It must display exactly 4 distinct channel plots (typically labeled 1, 2, 3, and 4 on the y-axis). There should not be 8 or 16 channels.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI application
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshot shows the genuine OpenBCI GUI application interface, not a terminal window, text editor, or a web browser showing a tutorial.

integrity.description: The data appears to be genuinely synthetic
integrity.visual_evidence: If waveforms are visible, they should look synthetic/algorithmic (e.g., clean sine/square waves or uniform generated noise), consistent with OpenBCI's Synthetic mode, rather than real noisy EEG recordings.

integrity.description: The screenshot is a genuine capture of the desktop/application
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no signs of image manipulation, such as pasted text, cropped UI elements from different versions, or fake overlays.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 should be running. When configured for the Ganglion board in Synthetic mode, the GUI generates algorithmic waveforms (not real EEG data). The Time Series widget must show exactly 4 channels, as Ganglion is a 4-channel board (unlike the default Cyton which has 8). Look for indicators of 'Ganglion' and 'Synthetic' in the GUI's top status bar or System Control Panel. The session must be actively running, which is typically indicated by a 'Stop Data Stream' button at the top of the interface.
